# Bridge WBTC
> An independent, non-custodial informational dashboard for understanding and comparing routes that transfer Wrapped Bitcoin between supported blockchain networks.

## What is Bridge WBTC
Bridge WBTC means moving Wrapped Bitcoin from one blockchain to another without changing its BTC-denominated asset identity. WBTC is a multichain token backed 1:1 by BTC held by authorized custodians. An informational dashboard displays routes, fees, timing, token contracts, and risks while the user retains wallet control. Bridging differs from a same-chain transfer, a swap into another token, and the custodian-mediated process that converts native BTC into newly minted WBTC.

## How it works
1. Select the source chain, destination chain, WBTC amount, and receiving address.
2. The route provider quotes network fees, bridge or relayer charges, expected output, and estimated completion time.
3. Connect a self-custody wallet, verify both WBTC contracts, approve only the required amount, and submit the source-chain transaction.
4. Under the hood, the route locks or burns WBTC, then sends a verified cross-chain message; liquidity routes may instead pay the destination from an existing pool.
5. The destination contract mints, unlocks, or transfers WBTC to the recipient. Track both transactions and verify the received token contract.

## Key facts
- Ethereum WBTC uses ERC-20 on chain ID 1.
- Its Ethereum contract is `0x2260FAC5E5542a773Aa44fBCfeDf7C193bc2C599`.
- WBTC uses eight decimal places.
- The official registry identifies native deployments on Ethereum, Solana, TRON, BNB Chain, Base, Kava, and Osmosis.
- Token standards and contract addresses differ by network.
- WBTC backing remains custodial even when the bridge interface is non-custodial.
- LayerZero OFT routes debit WBTC on the source and credit it on the destination while preserving unified supply.
- Total cost can include source gas, destination execution, relayer fees, bridge fees, and liquidity slippage.
- Completion ranges from minutes to days; OP Stack standard withdrawals to Ethereum have a seven-day challenge period.

## Common questions
### Is bridging WBTC safe?
Safety depends on the verified token contracts, bridge security model, destination chain, and WBTC custody system. Fake tokens, malicious approvals, contract exploits, message-verification failures, and depegging are primary risks.

### Who holds my funds?
The dashboard does not hold funds or private keys. Route contracts or liquidity providers handle the transfer, while authorized WBTC custodians hold the BTC reserves.

### What fees will I pay?
Expect source-chain gas plus any bridge, relayer, destination-execution, or liquidity fee shown in the quote. Keep each chain’s native gas token available.

### How long does it take?
Liquidity and messaging routes commonly finish after source and destination confirmations. Canonical optimistic-rollup withdrawals can require the full challenge period.

### Which chains are supported?
Support is route-specific. Confirm the route is live and compare every displayed token address with the official WBTC contract registry.

### How do I start?
Connect a compatible self-custody wallet, choose both networks, verify the recipient and token contracts, review the quote, approve WBTC, and sign the bridge transaction.

### What are the main risks?
The main risks are bridge exploits, custodian failure, incorrect networks or addresses, counterfeit WBTC, unlimited approvals, insufficient destination gas, low liquidity, and delayed finality.

## App
- Dashboard: https://bridge-wbtc.github.io/
- App: https://bridge-wbtc.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
WBTC Website: https://wbtc.network/
WBTC Documentation: https://docs.wbtc.network/

Mint and Burn Mechanism: https://docs.wbtc.network/how-wbtc-works/mint-burn-mechanism
Custody and Security: https://docs.wbtc.network/how-wbtc-works/custody-and-security
Proof of Reserves: https://wbtc.network/transparency
Audit Directory: https://docs.wbtc.network/how-wbtc-works/audits-and-smart-contract-security

WBTC DAO Source: https://github.com/WrappedBTC/DAO
Solana WBTC Source: https://github.com/solana-labs/wbtc
Official X: https://x.com/WrappedBTC
